text: The system shall merge or close all open pull requests before initiating repository transfer.
The system shall preserve pull requests during repository transfer.
Reviewers shall lose access to pull requests if they aren't added to the new organization.
The system shall document all repository secrets that need re-creation in the new organization.
The system shall store secret values in 1Password.
Projects V2 owned by user accounts shall not transfer automatically during repository transfer.
The system shall export each project's configuration including fields, views, and item assignments.
The system shall verify all CI workflows pass on main branch before migration.
The system shall list all GitHub Apps installed on the repository.
The system shall list all webhooks configured on the repository.
The system shall document branch protection rules.
The system shall delete stale branches before migration.
The system shall remove leftover git worktrees before migration.
The system shall verify no draft releases or pending deployments exist before migration.
The system shall create the GitHub organization through Settings > Organizations > New.
The system shall configure organization-level settings including member permissions and default repository visibility.
The system shall invite collaborators who need access to the new organization.
The system shall transfer repository ownership through repo Settings > Danger Zone > Transfer ownership.
GitHub shall set up a redirect from the old repository URL to the new organization repository.
Repository secrets shall not transfer during repository ownership transfer.
The system shall re-create all secrets in the new organization's repository settings.
The system shall update hardcoded repository URLs in tracked files.
The system shall create organization-level projects or re-link user projects.
The system shall recreate project fields, views, and filters from exported configuration.
The system shall re-assign issues and pull requests to the new projects.
The system shall install GitHub Apps on the new organization.
The system shall reconfigure webhooks that pointed to the old repository URL.
The system shall trigger a CI run on main branch after migration.
The system shall confirm all workflows pass with the new secrets.
The system shall enable Issue Types in the organization settings.
The system shall create issue types: Epic, Phase, Step, and Bug.
The system shall re-type existing issues to use new types instead of labels.
The system shall enable native blocking/blocked-by relationships.
The system shall migrate existing blocked label and body text conventions to native blocks.
The system shall remove the blocked label once migration is complete.
The system shall enable Issue Fields if available.
The system shall define typed metadata fields for priority, component, and effort.
The system shall configure default labels for the organization.
The system shall set up team-based code ownership using CODEOWNERS.
The system shall configure member permission levels.
All CI workflows shall be green on main branch after migration.
Scheduled workflows shall trigger on schedule after migration.
GitHub Actions minutes shall be tracked under the organization.
All Projects V2 views shall show correct data after migration.
Issue hierarchy including sub-issues and parent-child relationships shall remain intact.
Issue type assignments shall display correctly after migration.
Old repository URLs shall redirect to the new organization repository.
Pull request cross-references in issue bodies shall still resolve after migration.
Collaborators shall have correct permissions in the new organization.
Secrets shall be accessible in CI workflows after migration.
GitHub Apps shall function correctly after migration.
Webhooks shall fire correctly after migration.
Local git remotes shall be updated to the new repository URL.
Git push and git pull operations shall work against the new remote.
Local scripts and aliases that reference the old URL shall be updated.
The system must run training on local Linux machines
The system must run training on local macOS machines
The system must run training in Docker containers
The system must run training on RunPod
Checkpoints must survive pod death
The system must resume from the last durable checkpoint with full optimizer state
The system must resume from the last durable checkpoint with full scheduler state
Dataset version must be inspectable
Train config must be inspectable
Git SHA must be inspectable
Checkpoint lineage must be inspectable
The system must use a single-job mental model
Pod death must lose at most one checkpoint interval of progress
The same experiment must run locally, in Docker, and on RunPod
Every run must record dataset information
Every run must record config information
Every run must record SHA information
Every run must record artifact lineage
Local smoke test must reach checkpoint and exit cleanly
Crash recovery must provide one documented command to resume
Training must emit checkpoints on checkpoint cadence
WandbLogger with log_model='all' must upload after each local save
Resume must work with local checkpoint paths
Resume must work with wandb artifact references
Training must produce model artifact for promotion
Training must produce lineage for promotion
Training must produce config metadata for promotion
Training must not perform promotion
Dataset access must use local-only by default
If r2_path is specified, the system must sync dataset before training
Every training run must set job_type='training'
Every training run must call run.use_artifact() for the dataset artifact
Every training run must call run.log_artifact() for the model artifact
Every training run must include github_sha in wandb.config
Model artifact naming must follow the format model-{train_config_id}
Dataset artifact naming must follow the format data-{dataset_config_id}
Training must produce final metrics in wandb.summary
RunPod pods must auto-terminate after training exits
The system must support single-GPU for next-generation models
The system must generate dataset_config_id from the config filename stem in configs/dataset/{id}.yaml
The system must generate dataset_wandb_run_id with default format {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ} when not explicitly configured
The system must generate train_config_id from the config filename stem in configs/experiment/.../{id}.yaml
The system must generate train_wandb_run_id with default format {train_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ} when not explicitly configured
The system must generate eval_config_id from the eval dataset config filename stem in configs/dataset/{id}.yaml
The system must generate eval_wandb_run_id with default format {eval_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ} when not explicitly configured
The system must use timestamp format YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ (seconds, UTC, filesystem-safe) for default ID generation
The system must enforce W&B run ID limit of 64 characters
The system must store data in R2 path synth-data/data/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/
The system must store training outputs in R2 path synth-data/train/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/
The system must store evaluation outputs in R2 path synth-data/eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/
Workers may only write under metadata/workers/ directory during data generation
The finalize process must write shards/ directory only after worker completion
All rclone operations must use --checksum flag
Datasets must be immutable once dataset.complete marker exists
New dataset versions must require a new dataset_wandb_run_id
The system must create W&B dataset artifacts with name pattern data-{dataset_config_id}
The system must create W&B model artifacts with name pattern model-{train_config_id}
The system must create W&B eval-results artifacts with name pattern eval-{eval_config_id}
W&B must auto-version artifacts with :v0, :v1, :v2 format
The system must store wandb_run_id in artifact.metadata, not in the artifact name
W&B must automatically set :latest alias on every log_artifact call
Training scripts must set :best alias when validation metric improves using run.log_artifact(art, aliases=["best"])
Promote workflow must set :production alias when model is promoted to GitHub Release
Every run must call run.use_artifact() for inputs to create lineage links
Every run must call run.log_artifact() for outputs to create lineage links
Every run must include github_sha in wandb.config
The system must use run.use_artifact() instead of api.artifact() to create lineage links
The system must store hyperparameters (things set before the run) in wandb.config
The system must store final metrics (things measured after the run) in wandb.summary
The system must store artifact properties in artifact.metadata
Dataset properties must belong on the dataset artifact, not on training runs that consume it
The system must set job_type='data-generation' for data pipeline runs
The system must set job_type='training' for training runs
The system must set job_type='evaluation' for evaluation runs
All workflows that create W&B runs must export GITHUB_SHA into the run environment
Evaluation workflow must require train_wandb_run_id to find the model artifact
Evaluation workflow must require eval_config_id to determine which dataset to evaluate on
Promotion workflow must require both train_wandb_run_id and eval_wandb_run_id
Promotion workflow must pull model artifact from training run and eval metrics from eval run
GitHub Release tags must use format model-v{N} with incrementing integer
Promotion workflow must attach model file (.pt or .onnx) as release asset
Promotion workflow must set :production alias on the model artifact in W&B
Secrets must only be available to workflows that require them
Training workflows must have R2 read access to download dataset shards
Training workflows must have R2 write access to upload checkpoints
The system must use WANDB_ENTITY=tinaudio for W&B identity
The system must use WANDB_PROJECT=synth-setter for W&B identity
New runs must not use legacy benhayes/synth-permutations W&B project
W&B artifacts must reference R2 objects via artifact.add_reference("s3://synth-data/...")
Environment must set AWS_ENDPOINT_URL or WANDB_S3_ENDPOINT_URL to R2 endpoint when calling add_reference or downloading reference artifacts
W&B artifacts must not duplicate large data files
Bulk data must live in R2 while W&B provides index and lineage graph
Each wandb_run_id must uniquely identify an immutable run output
Every run must log W&B artifacts for its outputs
Every run must consume W&B artifacts for its inputs
Every run must record github_sha in wandb.config
All configs must be frozen into the artifact or R2 storage path at run time
R2 paths must be append-only after completion markers exist
Runs must not consume data from R2 paths that lack their completion marker
The system must run the full evaluation pipeline (predict, render, metrics) on any developer machine or CI runner.
The system must fetch datasets from R2 and checkpoints from W&B on demand.
The system must replace all cluster-specific hardcoded paths in committed configs with Hydra defaults.
The system must work on local macOS dev machines, local Linux machines, Docker containers, and CI runners.
Environment differences must be handled by config, not by code forks.
Every make target must be safe to re-run (idempotent).
All rclone operations must use checksum verification.
The system must enable tracing from aggregated CSV to per-sample CSV to rendered audio to predicted parameters to checkpoint to training run to dataset.
Fresh clone to metrics CSV must complete in under 15 minutes for small fixture.
No hardcoded /data/scratch/ paths must exist in committed configs.
The predict stage must load a trained checkpoint and run inference on test dataset.
The predict stage must output predicted parameter tensors to pred-{batch_idx}.pt files.
The predict stage must output target audio tensors to target-audio-{batch_idx}.pt files.
The predict stage must output target parameter tensors to target-params-{batch_idx}.pt files.
The render stage must load predicted parameter tensors and decode them using ParamSpec.
The render stage must render audio through Surge XT VST plugin via pedalboard.
The render stage must output sample_{N}/pred.wav, sample_{N}/target.wav, sample_{N}/spec.png, and sample_{N}/params.csv files.
The render stage must use native display on macOS and Xvfb on headless Linux.
The metrics stage must compute MSS, wMFCC, SOT, and RMS metrics for each predicted-target audio pair.
The metrics stage must output metrics.csv with per-sample metrics and aggregated_metrics.csv with mean/std across samples.
The metrics stage must use ProcessPoolExecutor for parallel metric computation across samples.
When data.r2_path is explicitly provided, SurgeDataModule.prepare_data() must sync the dataset to data.dataset_root.
If r2_path is absent, the system must operate in local-only mode with no R2 dependency.
If dataset_root already has matching data (by checksum), no sync operation must occur.
r2_path must have no default value and require explicit opt-in.
Checkpoints must be stored in W&B artifacts, not R2.
Training must upload every checkpoint saved by ModelCheckpoint using log_model='all'.
Experiment configs must pin W&B artifact references using ${wandb:tinaudio/synth-setter/model-flow_simple:latest} syntax.
The wandb OmegaConf resolver must cache downloads to $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/.
If cached copy exists, no W&B API call must be made.
The wandb resolver must return the path to model.ckpt in the cached directory.
R2 eval artifacts must be uploaded to path eval/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id}/{train_config_id}/{train_wandb_run_id}/{eval_config_id}/{eval_wandb_run_id}/.
make upload-eval must use rclone sync with --checksum flag.
Each eval must create a W&B run that declares model checkpoint and dataset as input artifacts.
W&B eval runs must log summary metrics (mss, wmfcc, sot, rms).
W&B eval runs must reference R2 location for bulk artifacts using s3:// protocol.
renderscript.sh must auto-detect display availability rather than requiring configuration.
On macOS, renderscript.sh must use native display without Xvfb.
On headless Linux, renderscript.sh must launch Xvfb on :99 and export DISPLAY.
renderscript.sh must clean up Xvfb process on exit.
Checkpoint resolution must support three patterns: CLI arg, experiment config, and null for training.
CLI override must have highest priority in checkpoint resolution.
Experiment config checkpoint references must be stable per model variant.
Base eval.yaml must force explicit checkpoint specification with ckpt_path: ???.
OmegaConf must resolve ${wandb:...} lazily only when cfg.ckpt_path is first accessed.
All eval operations must be accessible as make targets.
make predict must map to python src/eval.py mode=predict.
make render must map to renderscript.sh or direct Python on macOS.
make metrics must map to python scripts/compute_audio_metrics.py.
make eval must run the full pipeline: predict, render, metrics.
make docker-eval must run docker run ... make eval.
W&B must store training metrics, checkpoints, eval summary metrics, and artifact lineage.
R2 must store datasets and eval bulk artifacts (predictions, audio, spectrograms, per-sample metrics).
Hydra config must store full frozen config at eval time for exact reproducibility.
The 19 SGE scripts in jobs/predict/ must remain as-is and not be maintained.
No new code must reference SGE.
All committed YAML configs must not contain /data/scratch paths.
dataset_root in data configs must use ${paths.data_dir}/{dataset_config_id}/{dataset_wandb_run_id} pattern.
.env.example must contain only R2 credentials and WANDB_API_KEY.
ckpt_path must not be stored in .env file.
make predict must work with portable Hydra config on local machines.
dataset_root must have sensible Hydra default and support CLI override.
Predict must fail fast with clear error if dataset not found.
make render must work on macOS with native display and Linux with Xvfb auto-detect.
Plugin and preset paths must default to plugins/ and presets/ directories.
Plugin and preset paths must be overridable via environment variables.
make metrics must work with pinned dependencies and clean output schema.
Metrics output must index per-sample metrics by directory name.
rclone_sync() utility must enforce --checksum flag on all operations.
rclone functions must read R2 config from RCLONE_CONFIG_R2_* environment variables.
rclone functions must raise subprocess.CalledProcessError on failure.
rclone functions must support dry-run mode via --dry-run flag passthrough.
SurgeDataModule must add optional r2_path field.
SurgeDataModule must call rclone_sync in prepare_data() when r2_path is specified.
R2 sync must run in prepare_data() before setup().
R2 download must log progress via structlog.
make upload-eval must push predictions, audio, and metrics to R2.
wandb.yaml must change log_model from true to 'all'.
src/utils/utils.py must add _wandb_resolver to register_resolvers().
Wandb resolver cache directory must be $PROJECT_ROOT/.cache/checkpoints/.
Cache directory must be gitignored.
log_model='all' must upload every saved checkpoint (every 5000 steps + best + last).
scripts/compute_audio_metrics.py must support --wandb-run flag for optional W&B logging.
Docker eval environment must use multi-stage Dockerfile: base → deps → VST plugin → Xvfb.
Xvfb must be baked into Docker image for headless operation.
Surge XT plugin must be installed in Docker image.
.env file must be mounted to Docker container for credentials only.
Output directory must be mounted as Docker volume.
GitHub Actions workflow must run predict → render → metrics on small fixture.
CI must use checked-in fixtures with no R2 credentials or network dependency.
CI must validate that predictions exist, audio renders, and metrics CSV has expected schema.
Test fixtures must include tiny.ckpt (~1 MB), fixture-shard.h5 (~5 MB), and sample audio files (~1 MB).
If fixtures grow past ~10 MB, they must be migrated to Git LFS.
docs/eval-runbook.md must document setup, credentials, make targets, Docker, and troubleshooting.
MSS metric must use three mel-scale windows: 10ms/5ms, 25ms/10ms, 100ms/50ms with 64, 128, 128 mels respectively.
MSS metric must compute L1 distance between mel spectrograms at each scale, averaged.
wMFCC metric must extract 13 MFCCs and compute DTW alignment cost between sequences.
wMFCC metric must weight by frame energy where loud frames matter more.
SOT metric must compute STFT, normalize to probability distributions, and compute Wasserstein-1 distance.
SOT metric must average Wasserstein distance across frames.
RMS metric must compute cosine similarity between RMS envelopes in overlapping windows.
RMS metric must return values in range [-1, 1] where 1 means identical envelope shape.
Each phase must land through one or more PRs with the unit of planning being the Phase issue
Phase 1 must enable `make train EXPERIMENT=...` to work on local machine
Phase 1 must remove all `/data/scratch` paths from training configs
Phase 1 must make W&B entity/project configurable via environment variables
Phase 1 must create a training smoke test
Phase 2 must enable W&B `log_model="all"` configuration
Phase 2 must make checkpoints appear as W&B model artifacts when `log_model="all"` is enabled
Phase 2 must support resume functionality with `ckpt_path=wandb:model-{train_config_id}:latest`
Phase 2 must restore Lightning optimizer state correctly during resume
Phase 3 must enable `make runpod-train EXPERIMENT=...` to launch training pod
Phase 3 launcher must support checkpoint resume functionality
Phase 3 must ensure training exits cleanly when job completes
Phase 4 docker training run must succeed
Phase 4 CI fixture must reach first checkpoint
Phase 4 smoke test must require no secrets
Phase 1 must modify configs/data/*.yaml files
Phase 1 must modify configs/logger/wandb.yaml file
Phase 1 must modify Makefile
Phase 1 must modify src/train.py file
Phase 1 must create tests/test_train_smoke.py file
Phase 2 must create src/utils/wandb_checkpoint.py file
Phase 2 must create tests/test_wandb_checkpoint.py file
Phase 3 must create scripts/runpod_train.py file
Phase 3 must create tests/test_runpod_launcher.py file
Phase 4 must create docker/train/Dockerfile file
Phase 4 must create .github/workflows/train-smoke.yml file
Phase 4 must create tests/test_train_e2e.py file
Phase 5 must create docs/training-runbook.md file
The system must support reproducible pipeline with full provenance where re-running from the same spec on the same hardware and Docker image produces an identical dataset
The system must require minimal hand-holding with only two commands needed and no babysitting required
The system must provide debugability where failures are easy to find and understand
The system must maintain low cost with cheap GPUs, free egress, and no infrastructure to manage
The system must provide crash resilience to handle errors and crashes from data generation code
The system must be safe and resumable where every command is safe to run at any time in any order
The system must validate auth before compute to verify all credentials before launching any workers
The system must support local compute mode for development, unit tests, and integration tests
The system must use storage as the source of truth where shard completeness is determined by file existence and validation
The system must use reconciliation over orchestration by comparing desired state against actual state
The system must use deterministic work identity where shard IDs are logical and not tied to infrastructure
The system must provide stage isolation where each stage is an independent, reconcilable transform
The system must fail visibly where errors are captured, structured, and surfaced, never swallowed
The system must validate at boundaries where data is verified when entering and leaving each stage
The system must achieve zero manual intervention from generate to finalize
The system must achieve 500k samples in under 2 hours with 10 workers
The system must provide 100% of shard failures with actionable error messages
The system must support retry that re-generates only missing/invalid shards
The system must cost less than $5 for a 500k-sample dataset
The system must support multi-terabyte datasets
The system must have two stages: Generate and Finalize
The Generate stage must take Config YAML or spec as input and produce HDF5 shards in staging as output
The Finalize stage must take validated staged shards as input and produce format-dependent outputs plus stats.npz, dataset.json, and dataset.complete
The system must support HDF5 output format that produces train.h5, val.h5, test.h5 as HDF5 virtual datasets
The system must support WebDataset output format that produces train-{shard}.tar, val-{shard}.tar, test-{shard}.tar as WebDataset archives
Each stage must read from a well-defined storage input prefix
Each stage must write to a well-defined storage output prefix
Each stage must be able to determine its own completeness by inspecting storage
Each stage must be re-runnable at any time without affecting other stages
The system must use R2 as both data plane and control plane
The system must validate credentials for R2 and RunPod before launching workers
The generate command must read or create spec, list staged shards, validate staged shards, compute missing set, partition across N workers, submit N tasks, and exit

The finalize command must read spec, check for dataset.complete, list staged shards, structural-check staged shards, promote to canonical prefix, download shards, reshard, compute stats, register in W&B, upload, and write dataset.complete
The status command must show shard counts without data loading or re-validation
Workers must run full 4-check validation including structural, shape, value, and row count checks
Generate and status commands must use existence check by checking for .h5 and .valid marker in staging directory
Finalize must use structural check including valid HDF5 file, expected datasets present, and shapes match spec
A shard must be staged-valid when both .h5 file and .valid marker exist in the staging directory
The .valid marker must be the commit point for staged shards
A shard must be finalized when it has been promoted to data/shards/shard-{id}.h5 by finalize
Shard IDs must be logical and deterministic using the pattern shard-000000.h5 through shard-{max}.h5
Workers must write to per-attempt filenames using {worker_id}-{attempt_uuid}.h5 pattern
Workers must write .rendering marker at start of shard generation
Workers must write .valid marker only after render, validation, upload, and bookkeeping are complete
Workers must write .invalid marker and upload to quarantine on validation failure
Finalize must write .promoted marker after promoting staged shard to canonical prefix
Workers must only write under metadata/workers/ prefix
Finalize must only write to data/ prefix plus .promoted markers
Generate must determine missing work by inspecting storage and comparing spec against staged valid shards
Generate must exit with code 0 when nothing is missing
Finalize must exit with code 0 when dataset.complete exists and all canonical outputs exist
Finalize must exit with code 1 when any shards are missing
Finalize must exit with code 1 when any staged shards fail structural check
Each shard must render in a separate OS process using spawn context
Worker process must catch non-zero exit codes from child processes and mark shards as invalid
Bash EXIT trap must upload debug log and fallback error JSON if worker process dies
Workers must produce three artifacts: staged shard with lifecycle markers, worker report, and debug log
ComputeBackend must have submit method that takes image and task specs and returns submitted tasks
LocalBackend must run worker loop in-process with local filesystem as R2 equivalent
RunPodBackend must wrap the runpod Python SDK and map tasks to workers
R2 must provide strong read-after-write consistency for PUT then GET operations
R2 must provide strong read-after-write consistency for PUT then LIST operations
R2 must provide strong read-after-write consistency for DELETE then GET operations
All rclone operations must use --checksum flag to verify transfer integrity
Workers must validate shards locally before uploading to prevent corrupt data in staging
Workers must upload corrupt shards directly to quarantine directory with .invalid marker
Multiple attempts for the same shard must be visible in the staging directory
Finalize must select the lexicographically smallest worker-attempt filename when multiple valid attempts exist
Dataset.complete must be written as the very last step after all outputs are uploaded and verified
Finalize must restart from scratch if dataset.complete exists but outputs are missing or corrupt
All pipeline artifacts must use unique filenames per attempt to prevent overwrites
W&B must be initialized with project synth-setter and job_type data-generation
Pipeline metrics must be written to wandb.summary including shards_total, shards_valid, shards_quarantined, total_samples, generation_time_seconds, finalize_time_seconds, and errors_total
Dataset artifact must be registered with name data-{dataset_config_id} and type dataset
Dataset artifact must include input_spec.json and dataset.json files
Dataset artifact must include metadata with dataset_config_id, dataset_wandb_run_id, shard_count, param_spec, code_version, is_repo_dirty, and total_samples
Dataset artifact must reference R2 path to actual HDF5 data without uploading to W&B
Input spec must be frozen and immutable after creation

Input spec must include run_id, created_at, code_version, is_repo_dirty, param_spec, renderer_version, output_format, sample_rate, shard_size, base_seed, num_params, splits, plugin_path, preset_path, velocity, signal_duration_seconds, min_loudness, sample_batch_size, and shards
Per-shard seeds must be computed deterministically as base_seed + shard_id

Dataset card must include schema_version, run_id, finalized_at, code_version, is_repo_dirty, param_spec, renderer_version, output_format, sample_rate, total_samples, splits, stats, validation_summary, worker_architectures, input_spec_sha256, and input_spec_path
Worker report must include worker_id, attempt_uuid, assigned_shards, results, errors, started_at, completed_at, cpu_arch, and os_info
Each shard result must include shard_id, filename, rows, success, content_hash, render_time_sec, and error fields
Config materialization must extract renderer_version from plugin bundle
Config materialization must derive dataset_wandb_run_id as {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
Config materialization must create git diff and upload to metadata/run_diff.patch when repo is dirty
Generate must error if --config is passed for run_id that already has a spec
All structured data must use Pydantic BaseModel in strict mode
All transient storage operations must use tenacity retry policy with exponential backoff
Debug logs must use structlog with JSON rendering for structured events
Debug logs must be append-only and uploaded by EXIT trap to survive crashes

The system SHALL organize work using GitHub's native issue tracking with Issue Types (Epic, Phase, Task, Bug, Feature), native blocking, sub-issues for hierarchy, Projects V2 for views, and milestones for releases.
Each work stream SHALL follow the pattern: design doc → Epic → Phases → Tasks.
The system SHALL classify issues using GitHub's native Issue Types at the org-level with types: Epic, Phase, Task, Bug, and Feature.
Issue types SHALL be set on the issue itself, be filterable in issue lists, and show distinct icons.
Epic issues SHALL serve as umbrella issues grouping phases for a work stream.
Phase issues SHALL represent large feature areas within an epic.
Task issues SHALL represent units of work that can be standalone or within a phase.
Bug issues SHALL represent something that isn't working.
Feature issues SHALL represent requests, ideas, or new functionality.
All work streams SHALL use the hierarchy structure: Epic → Phase → Task via native sub-issues.
Phase issues SHALL be sub-issues of their Epic.
Task issues under a phase SHALL be sub-issues of that Phase.
The system SHALL support hierarchy tracking via native sub-issues up to 8 levels deep.
Projects SHALL render hierarchy as an expandable tree via hierarchy view.
Phase issues SHALL be named using the pattern 'Phase N: Name'.
Task issues under phases SHALL be named using the pattern 'Task N.M: Name'.
All PRs SHALL merge to the main branch.
Tasks within a phase SHALL be independently testable.
The system SHALL track blocking via GitHub's native dependency system using 'Mark as blocked by' and 'Mark as blocking' options.
Blocked issues SHALL show a Blocked icon in project boards and issue lists.
Dependencies SHALL be machine-readable via the GraphQL API through blockedBy and blocking fields.
Tasks that modify the same files within the same work stream SHALL be sequenced to avoid merge conflicts.

The system SHALL track priority via a Priority single-select field on the project with values: P0 (Blocks all progress), P1 (Needed before milestone ships), P2 (Planned but not blocking), P3 (Nice-to-have).
Labels SHALL classify issues by domain only.
Most issues SHALL carry a single domain label.
Cross-cutting infrastructure issues MAY carry multiple domain labels only when the work genuinely spans work streams.
Every work stream SHALL have a corresponding milestone.
Milestones SHALL be set on each issue individually.
The system SHALL use a single org-level GitHub Project containing all issues.
The project SHALL have custom fields: Priority, Start Date, and Target Date.
The project SHALL support status transitions: Todo → In Progress → Done → Verified.
Won't Fix SHALL be a terminal status reachable from any prior status.
Verified SHALL be the final status for completed work indicating the work is done and verified working as expected.
The system SHALL use saved views with domain label filters to switch between work streams.
Design doc headers SHALL reference tracking issues using the format 'Tracking: #N (description)'.
Issue bodies SHALL reference design docs using the format 'Design doc: filename §section'.

The system SHALL support the issue lifecycle: set Issue Type → add domain label → assign milestone → add to project → set Priority → add blocking dependencies → move to In Progress → link PR → move to Done → verify and move to Verified → close issue.
PRs that fully resolve an issue SHALL use 'Fixes #N' or 'Closes #N' for auto-closing.
PRs that are related but don't resolve an issue SHALL use 'Refs #N'.
Issues SHALL be moved to Done when the PR merges but kept open until verified.
Issues SHALL be moved to Verified and closed only after verifying the change works as expected.
Issues that are intentionally declined SHALL be moved to Won't Fix and closed with a reason comment.
The migration SHALL require creating a single org-level project with specified fields and saved views.
All issues SHALL be added to the project with epic issues added explicitly and sub-issues appearing automatically via hierarchy view.
Issue types SHALL be set on all existing issues via sidebar or GraphQL API.
Existing blocking relationships SHALL be migrated to native dependencies using GraphQL API.
Priority SHALL be set on all project items from existing label data.
Retired labels SHALL be deleted except for 'bug' which is kept for Dependabot use.
Implementation tasks must have integration and unit tests written before implementation using TDD
Infrastructure tasks must be verified via CI green status and successful Docker builds
Each task must correspond to one PR
CI must validate every PR to maintain always-working pipeline
Pipeline directory must be at project root and invoked via python -m pipeline
Tests must be located in tests/pipeline/ with own conftest.py
Storage layout, IDs, and W&B conventions must follow storage-provenance-spec.md
Phase 1 CI gate must pass pytest and ruff clean
Phase 2 CI gate must pass pytest tests/pipeline/
Phase 3 CI gate must succeed Docker build and BATS
Phase 4 CI gate must pass pytest tests/pipeline/
Phase 5 CI gate must pass full integration tests
Phase 6 CI gate must pass E2E test and adhoc Docker test
Config filenames must encode runtime parameters where filename stem is dataset_config_id
Run ID derivation must follow format dataset_wandb_run_id = {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
Renderer version must be auto-extracted at materialization from VST3 bundle
System must fallback to unknown renderer version with warning if extraction fails
HDF5 output format must produce virtual HDF5 datasets for train.h5, val.h5, test.h5
WDS output format must produce WebDataset tar archives for multi-GPU streaming
Generation must always produce HDF5 shards regardless of output format
WDS must be a finalize transcoding step from HDF5 shards
Requirements-app.txt must include pydantic, h5py, click, runpod, wandb, structlog, tenacity, webdataset, numpy
Requirements-torch.txt must include torch index URL and packages
Pyproject.toml must include pipeline pytest marker
Hdf5plugin must be included in dependencies for Blosc2-compressed virtual datasets
Task 5.3 finalize and all HDF5 tests must import hdf5plugin
Mutmut must be added to dev dependencies for verification strategy
Uploader.py and minor fixes must be ported from experiment branch
All existing tests must still pass after porting core shared code
CI must run on every push and PR
CI must install dependencies from requirements.txt
CI must run ruff lint and pytest
CI must fail PR if any test fails
Env.example must include template with placeholder values for R2, W&B, and RunPod credentials
.env must be in .gitignore
No real credentials must be committed
DatasetConfig must use Pydantic strict validation for raw YAML input
Output_format must default to hdf5 if missing from config
DatasetPipelineSpec must be frozen and strict
DatasetPipelineSpec must include dataset_config_id, dataset_wandb_run_id, created_at, code_version, is_repo_dirty
DatasetPipelineSpec must validate that train + val + test equals num_shards
ShardSpec must include shard_id as int, filename, seed, row_start, row_count, expected_datasets, audio_shape, mel_shape, param_shape
Sample dataclass must be frozen with slots including sample_id, audio, mel_spec, params
Sample must use dataclass for internal typed containers, not Pydantic
ShardResult must include shard_id, filename, rows, success, content_hash, render_time_sec, error
WorkerReport must include cpu_arch, os_info, attempt_uuid, results list
DatasetCard must include schema_version, dataset_config_id, dataset_wandb_run_id, finalized_at, and other specified fields
Run ID format must follow {dataset_config_id}-{YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ}
Dataset_config_id must be the config filename stem
Materialize_spec function must derive all runtime state internally
Tests must mock I/O helpers instead of using optional overrides
Unit tests must cover construction, strict validation, immutability, JSON round-trip
Unit tests must verify materialize_spec produces correct shard count, deterministic seeds, zero-padded IDs
Unit tests must verify row partitioning without gaps or overlaps
Storage layer must abstract R2/local filesystem with design doc path layout
Storage layer must wrap src/data/uploader.py
Path computation must match storage-provenance-spec.md R2 layout
StorageBackend protocol must include list_shard_markers, write_marker, upload_file, download_file, list_prefix, exists
R2StorageBackend must include --checksum in every rclone command
R2StorageBackend must delegate to RcloneUploader.upload() for directory uploads
Unit tests must verify path generation matches storage-provenance-spec.md for all artifact types
Unit tests must verify local write → exists → list round-trip
R2 unit tests must verify rclone command construction with --checksum flag
Validation must implement 3-tier validation from design doc
Full validation must perform 4 checks: structural, shape, value, row count
Existence validation must check .h5 and .valid marker
Structural validation must open h5py, verify datasets present, verify shapes match
Validation must return ValidationResult with is_valid and checks list
Validation functions must be pure functions
_make_test_spec helper must return valid DatasetPipelineSpec with sensible defaults
Tests/pipeline/conftest.py must add project root to sys.path if needed
Unit tests must verify each validation tier catches exactly the failures it's responsible for
Docker build system must be ported from experiment branch
Make docker-build-dev-snapshot must build successfully
Existing entrypoint modes must work after porting
Reconcile function must return ReconciliationResult with missing, valid, rendering, invalid sets
Shard state must be determined from markers: .valid → valid, .rendering only → rendering, .invalid → invalid, nothing → missing
Multiple attempts must prioritize .valid marker
Partition_work must distribute shards across workers
Unit tests must cover all missing, all valid, mixed states, multiple attempts, partition edge cases
Each shard must render in a separate OS process via multiprocessing spawn context
Parent worker must spawn one child per shard and handle exit codes
Spawn context must start fresh Python interpreter per child
Per-shard lifecycle must write .rendering to remote storage FIRST
Child process must import and call make_dataset(shard_path, shard_spec)
Parent must wait with join(timeout=SHARD_TIMEOUT)
On success: validate locally → upload .h5 to storage → write .valid to storage
.rendering marker in remote storage must survive worker/child death
On validation failure or child crash: upload corrupt shard to quarantine/, write .invalid marker, log failure details
.rendering marker must remain after failure (append-only, never deleted)
Worker must check staging directory for existing .valid shard before spawning
Worker must produce WorkerReport with per-shard results, content hashes, timing, exit codes
Worker must create JSONL debug log via structlog file handler
Timed-out children must be killed with p.kill() and shard marked invalid
Each child process must use per-process X11 display number for Xvfb display isolation
Child process must import make_dataset directly, no generate_fn argument
LocalBackend must call run_worker() in-process for tests
ComputeBackend.submit must return list of SubmittedTask
TaskSpec model must include run_id, shards, spec
Unit tests must verify .rendering marker exists in storage before .valid marker
Unit tests must verify validation failure leads to .invalid marker and shard in quarantine/
Unit tests must test process crash isolation via real spawn path with SIGSEGV
Unit tests must test per-shard timeout with child killed after timeout
CLI must support python -m pipeline generate with config, workers, backend flags
CLI must support --storage-root flag for local path or env-based R2
CLI must support --log-level flag with default INFO
CLI must validate auth by checking R2 connectivity and RunPod API key before launching
CLI must provide clear error message and exit 1 on auth failure with no workers launched
CLI must validate plugin_path exists before materialization
First run must follow: config → validate → extract renderer_version → materialize spec → upload frozen spec → upload source config → reconcile → partition → submit → exit
CLI must print run_id prominently for user to use in status/finalize
Retry with --run-id must load spec → reconcile → submit missing → exit
CLI must error on --config with existing run_id (immutable spec)
--dry-run must print plan without submitting work
Unit tests must verify first run creates spec, retry loads existing, config drift error
Unit tests must verify --dry-run prints shard assignments, creates no spec, submits no work
Unit tests must verify auth validation failure produces clear error and exit 1 with no workers
Status command must provide read-only reconciliation report
Status command must support --json flag for machine-readable output
Status must overlay recent worker errors from metadata/workers/attempts/*/report.json
Status must exit 0 if all valid, 1 otherwise with no writes to storage
Status unit tests must verify all-valid exit 0, missing exit 1, invalid shows details, --json output parseable
Finalize command must validate staged → promote → download → stats → training outputs → dataset card
Finalize must support --output-dir, --skip-wandb, --keep-quarantine-days, --dry-run flags
Already-finalized must exit 0 (idempotent)
Stale dataset.complete must delete marker and re-run
Missing shards must cause exit 1
Finalize must structural-check each staged shard
Multiple attempts must pick lexicographically smallest worker_id-attempt_uuid filename
Finalize must promote to data/shards/ and write .promoted markers without deleting staged files
Finalize must compute stats FIRST, then produce training outputs based on output_format
HDF5 format must create virtual HDF5 datasets using VirtualLayout/VirtualSource pattern
WDS format must create WebDataset tar archives via Sample dataclass
Dataset card must include output_format, worker_architectures with warning if heterogeneous
Finalize must upload finalized outputs to R2 storage
Dataset.complete must contain dataset_wandb_run_id and timestamp, written last
W&B integration must log 7 specific metrics to wandb.summary
W&B must register dataset artifact as data-{dataset_config_id}
Unit tests must verify promotes, rejects missing/corrupt, idempotent, stale marker recovery
Unit tests must verify lexicographic shard selection with multiple attempts
Unit tests must verify .promoted markers written and dataset.complete content
Unit tests must verify both output formats and --dry-run functionality
Unit tests must include mock-W&B test verifying all 7 metrics logged
RunPodBackend must use runpod.create_pod() with env vars, auth check, dry-run
RunPodBackend must tag all pods with run_id for cleanup
Cleanup CLI command must query RunPod API for pods tagged with run_id and terminate them
Docker MODE=pipeline-worker must execute python -m pipeline.worker
Bash EXIT trap must upload JSONL debug log and fallback error.json on crash
Adhoc Docker script must build image, run container with test config, verify shard output
Unit tests must cover RunPod env vars with mocked API, missing key error, dry-run, pod tagging
Unit tests must verify cleanup command and BATS entrypoint functionality
EXIT trap test must verify log uploaded when process is killed
.valid marker must be the LAST write in the shard protocol
.rendering marker must be append-only and never deleted
Workers must never write to data/shards/ directory
Missing worker report must not block shard validity
Hard timeout in docker_entrypoint.sh must kill worker process after configurable max duration
EXIT trap must fire on SIGTERM timeout kill with debug log and error.json uploaded
RunPod must configure auto-stop in RunPodBackend.submit()
Generate command must support --timeout flag
BATS test must verify worker killed via SIGTERM after timeout still uploads debug log
Per-PR verification must run pytest and ruff on every push
After all tasks verification must run pytest tests/pipeline/ -v and pytest tests/pipeline/test_e2e.py -v
Local dry run verification must execute python -m pipeline generate with test config
Docker fidelity verification must run bash scripts/test_local_docker.sh
Mutation testing verification must run mutmut run --paths-to-mutate=pipeline/
The entrypoint dispatches on the MODE environment variable
The container errors if MODE is unset
When MODE is 'idle', the entrypoint executes 'sleep infinity' regardless of arguments
When MODE is 'passthrough' and arguments are given, the entrypoint executes the provided arguments
When MODE is 'passthrough' and no arguments are given, the entrypoint exits with code 0
When MODE is 'generate_dataset', the entrypoint runs VST dataset generation via pipeline.entrypoints.generate_dataset under headless X11
When MODE has an unknown value, the container errors
The generate_dataset mode uses environment variables instead of CLI arguments
The Dockerfile defines three targets: prod, dev-snapshot, and dev-live
The prod target uses docker_entrypoint.sh as entrypoint with source code baked at GIT_REF as tarball
The dev-snapshot target uses docker_entrypoint.sh as entrypoint with Git clone at GIT_REF
The dev-live target has a fallback entrypoint that errors without mount and uses volume-mounted source code
All targets inherit from r2-config-base
R2 credentials are baked only when BuildKit secrets are provided at build time
When BuildKit secrets are not provided, a placeholder rclone config is used
W&B auth is not baked into the image
WANDB_API_KEY is required at runtime
IMAGE build argument defaults to 'dev-snapshot'
IMAGE build argument selects the final target (prod, dev-snapshot, or dev-live)
SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF build argument defaults to 'main'
SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF specifies the Git ref for source code
BUILD_MODE build argument defaults to 'source'
BUILD_MODE accepts 'source' or 'prebuilt' values for Surge install method
TORCH_INDEX_URL build argument is required
SYNTH_PERMUTATIONS_GIT_REF environment variable is set in prod and dev-snapshot targets
R2_BUCKET environment variable is set in all targets via r2-config-base
VIRTUAL_ENV environment variable is set to '/venv/main' in all targets
PATH environment variable includes '$VIRTUAL_ENV/bin:$PATH' in all targets
DATASET_CONFIG environment variable is required for MODE=generate_dataset
DATASET_CONFIG specifies the path to dataset config YAML in container
RUN_METADATA_DIR environment variable defaults to '/run-metadata' for MODE=generate_dataset
RUN_METADATA_DIR specifies the directory where input_spec.json is written
The container materializes a DatasetPipelineSpec in generate_dataset mode
The container uploads spec and shard to R2 in generate_dataset mode
input_spec.json is written to RUN_METADATA_DIR in generate_dataset mode
The entrypoint generates shard_size samples (one shard per invocation)
Multi-shard generation (num_shards > 1) raises NotImplementedError
W&B runs must be created via Lightning's WandbLogger without direct wandb.init() calls in training or eval code
The WandbLogger must be instantiated via Hydra configuration and passed to the Trainer
Most metric logging must go through Lightning's self.log() / self.log_dict() API
Visualization callbacks may call wandb.log() directly for image uploads
The WandbLogger must be instantiated by Hydra configuration
Entity must be hardcoded as 'benhayes' in configs/logger/wandb.yaml:10
Project must be hardcoded as 'synth-permutations' in configs/logger/wandb.yaml:13
Run ID must be set to null to allow W&B auto-generation in configs/logger/wandb.yaml:8
Checkpoint upload must be enabled with log_model: true in configs/logger/wandb.yaml:11
Code saving must use wandb.Settings(code_dir='.') in configs/logger/wandb.yaml:17-19
Run teardown must call wandb.finish() in task_wrapper finally block in src/utils/utils.py:102-107
No direct wandb.init() or wandb.config.update() calls must exist in runtime code (src/, scripts/, entrypoints)
Hyperparameters must be logged once at run start via log_hyperparameters() in src/utils/logging_utils.py
The hyperparameters dict must be sent to all loggers via logger.log_hyperparams()
Hyperparameters must include model config subtree under 'model' key
Hyperparameters must include total parameter count under 'model/params/total' key
Hyperparameters must include trainable parameter count under 'model/params/trainable' key
Hyperparameters must include non-trainable parameter count under 'model/params/non_trainable' key
Hyperparameters must include data config subtree under 'data' key
Hyperparameters must include trainer config subtree under 'trainer' key
Hyperparameters must include callback config under 'callbacks' key
Hyperparameters must include extras config under 'extras' key
Hyperparameters must include task_name from config
Hyperparameters must include tags from config
Hyperparameters must include ckpt_path from config
Hyperparameters must include seed from config
Training metrics must be logged via self.log() in each LightningModule
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log train/loss per step and per epoch
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log train/penalty per step and per epoch
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log val/param_mse per epoch only
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log test/param_mse per epoch only
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log vector_field/*_norm per step only
SurgeFlowMatchingModule must log encoder/*_norm per step only
PlotLossPerTimestep callback must log plot images on validation_epoch_end to src/utils/callbacks.py:77
PlotPositionalEncodingSimilarity callback must log pos_enc_similarity images on validation_epoch_end to src/utils/callbacks.py:135
PlotLearntProjection callback must log assignment and value images on validation_epoch_end or every N steps to src/utils/callbacks.py:259
LogPerParamMSE callback must log per_param_mse/{name} per parameter on validation_epoch_end via self.log_dict to src/utils/callbacks.py:376-378
ModelCheckpoint callback must save .ckpt files locally for upload by log_model: true
If cfg.watch_gradients is set, watch_gradients() must call WandbLogger.watch(model, log='gradients')
watch_gradients() must be implemented in src/utils/utils.py:138-149
watch_gradients() must be called from src/train.py:89-91
Model checkpoints must be saved as artifacts via ModelCheckpoint + log_model: true
Source code must be saved as artifacts via wandb.Settings(code_dir='.') at run start
src/train.py must provide full W&B usage: logger init → hparams → train metrics → test metrics → teardown
src/eval.py must provide full W&B usage: logger init → hparams → test/val metrics → teardown
Both src/train.py and src/eval.py must use @task_wrapper to ensure wandb.finish() runs even on exception
Docker must have BuildKit enabled (Docker Desktop 23+ or DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1)
The .env file must contain secrets: GIT_PAT, R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_ENDPOINT, R2_BUCKET, WANDB_API_KEY
Credentials must be injected at build time via --mount=type=secret
Secret data must be available only during the RUN instruction and never appear in docker history
git_pat secret must not be persisted in the final image
r2_access_key_id secret must be persisted to /root/.config/rclone/rclone.conf
r2_secret_access_key secret must be persisted to /root/.config/rclone/rclone.conf
r2_endpoint secret must be persisted to /root/.config/rclone/rclone.conf
wandb_api_key secret must be persisted to /root/.netrc
Images with R2 and W&B credentials must only be pushed to private registries
R2 tokens must be rotated after each build campaign
dev-live image must include Surge XT and Python dependencies
dev-live image must not include baked source code
dev-live image must support mounting local working tree at runtime
make docker-build-dev-live must require GIT_PAT parameter
--load flag must import the built image into local Docker daemon
--push flag must push directly to a registry
dev-live must use a fallback entrypoint (not docker_entrypoint.sh)
dev-snapshot must clone source code at GIT_REF
Both dev-live and dev-snapshot targets must require GIT_PAT
dev-snapshot target must additionally require GIT_REF
DOCKER_FILE must default to docker/ubuntu22_04/Dockerfile
DOCKER_IMAGE must default to synth-setter
DOCKER_BUILD_MODE must default to prebuilt
DOCKER_TARGETPLATFORM must default to linux/amd64
DOCKER_TORCH_IDX must default to CUDA 12.8 wheels
DOCKER_BUILD_FLAGS must default to empty
DOCKER_TORCH_IDX can be set via torch_index_url in image config YAML
CLI takes precedence over YAML for DOCKER_TORCH_IDX
Image config files must be located under configs/image/
Image config must be validated by image_config.py Pydantic BaseModel
Image config BaseModel must use strict=True and extra='forbid'
Config loader must reject unknown keys, invalid types, and malformed values at load time
Only prod and dev-snapshot must include docker_entrypoint.sh with MODE dispatch
dev-live must have a fallback entrypoint that errors unless repo is mounted at /home/build/synth-setter
For dev-snapshot and prod, MODE must be required and container must exit with error if unset
There must be no default MODE to avoid silent misconfiguration
MODE=idle must provide a debug shell
MODE=passthrough must run a command or no-op (exit 0)
MODE=generate_dataset must generate VST dataset shard via generate_vst_dataset.py under headless X11
X11 bootstrapping must be handled by scripts/run-linux-vst-headless.sh
Entrypoint module pipeline/entrypoints/generate_dataset.py must read env/config and invoke generate_vst_dataset.py
Container must materialize a DatasetPipelineSpec and upload spec and shard to R2
input_spec.json must be written to RUN_METADATA_DIR
DATASET_CONFIG environment variable must be required for MODE=generate_dataset
RUN_METADATA_DIR must default to /run-metadata if not specified
Test workflow must upload artifact bundle named test-run-metadata
Artifact bundle must contain input_spec.json and generate.log files
Artifact retention must be 7 days
dev-live must require mounting working tree at /home/build/synth-setter
dev-live must require overriding the entrypoint
VST3 plugins must require an X11 display
Headless bootstrap script must start Xvfb automatically
GHA workflow must build dev-snapshot image and push to Docker Hub
Workflow must validate image config via Pydantic on all runs
Workflow must build image using Docker Buildx on all runs
Workflow must push tagged images to Docker Hub on dispatch/schedule only
Workflow must run smoke tests on dispatch/schedule only
Pull requests must run build validation only (no push, no smoke tests)
Workflow must fail before build starts if YAML violates schema
tinaudio/perm:dev-snapshot tag must be mutable for convenience
tinaudio/perm:dev-snapshot-<sha> tag must be immutable for smoke tests
Smoke tests must pull SHA-pinned tag to avoid race conditions
Multi-stage Dockerfile build can exceed 7 GiB RAM
Local Docker Desktop must allocate 16 GiB RAM for builds
GitHub Actions must use ubuntu-latest-4core (16 GiB) or larger runner
scripts/run-linux-vst-headless.sh must start Xvfb automatically
LIBGL_ALWAYS_SOFTWARE=1 must be set for software rendering
GHA workflow must use Docker Hub registry cache (type=registry)
Cache layers must be stored as tinaudio/perm:buildcache
Container must exit with 'MODE is required' error when MODE env var is not set
Container must exit with 'unknown MODE' error when MODE value is invalid
Valid MODE values must be 'idle', 'passthrough', and 'generate_dataset'
The promote script must accept a required --run-id argument for the W&B run ID to promote
The promote script must support WANDB_API_KEY environment variable for W&B authentication
The promote script must support GH_TOKEN environment variable for GitHub CLI authentication
The promote script must default WANDB_ENTITY to 'tinaudio' if not provided
The promote script must default WANDB_PROJECT to 'synth-setter' if not provided
The get_next_version_tag function must determine the next model-vN tag by listing existing releases
The get_next_version_tag function must return 'model-v1' if no existing model-v releases are found
The get_next_version_tag function must increment the highest existing model-v version number by 1
The get_previous_release_metrics function must pull metrics from the most recent release body for delta comparison
The format_release_body function must create a GitHub Release body with an eval card containing W&B run, date, and git SHA
The format_release_body function must include a metrics table with all non-underscore-prefixed summary values
The format_release_body function must format float metrics to 6 decimal places
The format_release_body function must include the run config as formatted JSON
The format_release_body function must include a dataset table listing input artifacts with type, name, and version
The download_model_artifact function must download model artifacts from the run and return the local path
The download_model_artifact function must prioritize .pt, .onnx, and .pth files when multiple files are downloaded
The download_model_artifact function must print a warning if no model artifact is found on the run
The download_model_artifact function must print a warning if multiple model artifacts are found and use the first one
The promote function must link model artifacts to W&B registry with the release tag and 'latest' aliases when registry_path is provided
The promote function must create a GitHub release using the gh CLI with the generated tag, title, and body
The promote function must attach the downloaded model file to the GitHub release
The promote function must set GITHUB_OUTPUT environment variables RELEASE_TAG and RELEASE_URL when running in CI
The promote function must only print the release body without creating it when dry_run is true
The promote function must exit with code 1 if the gh release create command fails
The GitHub Actions workflow must trigger on workflow_dispatch with required run_id input
The GitHub Actions workflow must support optional registry and dry_run inputs
The GitHub Actions workflow must have contents: write permissions to create releases
The GitHub Actions workflow must run on ubuntu-latest
The GitHub Actions workflow must checkout the repository code
The GitHub Actions workflow must setup Python 3.10
The GitHub Actions workflow must install the wandb package
The GitHub Actions workflow must pass WANDB_API_KEY and GH_TOKEN secrets to the promote script
The GitHub Actions workflow must create a step summary with release tag, URL, and W&B run ID when not in dry run mode
